title: Astroloma divaricatum
taxonomy: Astroloma divaricatum belongs to the family Ericaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Astroloma, which consists of several species of small, shrub-like plants native to Australia. The Ericaceae family is commonly known as the heath family, and it includes a wide range of plants found in various parts of the world. These plants often have small, often white or pink flowers and are commonly found in acidic soils. The classification of Astroloma divaricatum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other members of the Ericaceae family.

intro: Astroloma divaricatum, commonly known as the divaricate astroloma or divaricating astroloma, is a low-growing, shrubby plant species belonging to the family Ericaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Astroloma divaricatum is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as eczema and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Astroloma divaricatum is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema and dermatitis, as well as to aid in wound healing and tissue repair.
